summary.intent: Push a feature branch (t3code/497b39cd) containing snapshot fixtures to ars-ui, open PR #675, and complete the mandatory Codex AI review cycle by addressing all review findings before the PR is mergeable.
summary.outcome: PR #675 was opened with the snapshot-reviewed label, Codex returned 5 concrete state-machine edge case findings, all were fixed and validated (form reset, error-message tracking, focused state cleanup, roving tabindex fallback), and a follow-up commit was pushed with full CI green.

repo: Codex review is triggered via @codex review comment and tracked by watching GitHub reactions (👀 = in-progress, +1 = approved) plus unresolved review threads from the codex user via GraphQL API.
PRs introducing .snap fixture files require the snapshot-reviewed label.

Validation gate before committing review fixes: cargo +nightly fmt --all --check, cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo test --test spec_conformance, cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject, cargo xtask spec validate, and cargo llvm-cov for touched modules.
Form reset events (Event::Reset) must bypass editability guards in group components; this is intentional form semantics, not a side-effect of the controlled/uncontrolled state machine.
RadioGroup must explicitly track whether an error message child has been rendered (SetHasError / SetHasDescription signals) to correctly set aria-describedby.
Roving tabindex in RadioGroup must fall back gracefully when a controlled selected key refers to a disabled or missing item.
Focused state must be cleared when a prop change makes an item unfocusable (e.g., disabled while focused).

code.finding: Reset handling logic; form reset must bypass editability guards.
Additional reset/default value wiring patched for Codex finding.
Roving tabindex step/focus logic; fallback when selected key is stale or disabled.
first_enabled / idle_focus_seed helpers; used by roving tabindex fallback path.
RegisterItem / service() wiring; a registration-order bug caused an off-by-one in the focused-state cleanup that needed patching.
Spec tests for SetHasDescription / aria-describedby behavior updated to match new stricter error-message gating.

workflow: Polling Codex review status requires two independent signals: GitHub reactions on the issue (👀 drops when review ends) AND unresolved GraphQL review threads authored by codex — neither alone is sufficient.
cargo +nightly fmt must be run after writing new test bodies; line-wrapping violations are not caught by the editor and will fail CI.
Removing stale .snap.new files (rm -f ...snap.new) is required after manually adjusting snapshot expectations, otherwise cargo insta rejects them as unreferenced.
Running targeted llvm-cov (--lib -- <module>) rather than full coverage is sufficient to satisfy the coverage gate for a fix-only commit touching specific modules.

friction: Codex review took an extended polling period (~15+ minutes) with no intermediate feedback, requiring a busy-wait loop querying GitHub GraphQL every 120 seconds.
cargo +nightly fmt line-wrapping violations in new test code were only caught after all other validation gates had already been submitted, requiring a rerun.
Several intermediate write_stdin calls suggest the interactive commit hook process was slow and non-deterministic, adding latency at the start of the session.
Snapshot adjustments were needed after the stricter error-message gating change, requiring manual .snap.new cleanup in addition to re-running insta.
open_items: PR #675 still needs full CI completion and final merge; the session ends after the fix commit is pushed but before merge.
Codex unresolved threads need to be replied to inline and resolved — the assistant stated intent to do this but the transcript ends before confirmation.
Coverage for the broader spec_conformance suite was not re-run after the fix commit; only module-targeted llvm-cov was validated.
